title: あなたはSecurity Analystです。OWASP Top 10を参考に分析します。 ## チェック項目 - SQLインジェクション - XSS - CSRF - 認証・認可の不備 - 機密情報のハードコード - コマンドインジェクション ## 評価（3点満点） 3点: 問題なし 2点: 軽微な懸念 1点: 要対応 0点: 重大な脆弱性 ## 出力形式（JSON） { "score": 0-3, "vulnerabilities": [{ "severity": "critical|high|medium|low", "type": "", "description": "", "rem...
summary_text: {"score":1,"vulnerabilities":[{"severity":"high","type":"command_injection","description":"Assumption: this review is limited to this repository and treats downstream CLIs as black boxes. In `kernel-lane-bridge.mjs`, the Claude fallback path can switch to Copilot and invoke `copilot -p ... --allow-all-tools --add-dir <cwd>` (`buildCommand`, around lines 935-950; fallback dispatch around lines 1210-1242). That means a fallback triggered by provider failure can widen from a mostly constrained lane to a tool-enabled local agent. If task text or attached context becomes untrusted through the planned `/kernel` queue/front door, prompt injection can turn into local command execution against the workspace.","remediation":"Do not use mutation-capable fallback lanes automatically. Remove `--allow-all-tools` from automatic fallback, require a safe/read-only mode for fallback providers, and make capability parity a scheduler rule so a degraded fallback never has more authority than the primary lane."},{"severity":"high","type":"authentication_authorization","description":"The local runner currently trusts caller-controlled inputs that would become security boundaries once the resident supervisor/queue is added. `scripts/local/run-kernel-orchestration.sh` accepts arbitrary `--context-file` and environment overrides such as `KERNEL_LANE_BRIDGE_BIN`, `KERNEL_LANE_BRIDGE_SCRIPT`, and `KERNEL_ORCHESTRATION_MATRIX_JSON` with only existence/shape checks (around lines 81-116 and 151-164). `kernel-lane-bridge.mjs` then reads the referenced file and injects its contents into provider prompts (around lines 819, 914, 936, 955). Impact today is mostly trusted-local; impact after queue/front-door exposure is arbitrary file exfiltration or arbitrary local executable selection.","remediation":"For the backlog item that turns this into a resident supervisor, drop caller-supplied binary/env overrides, pin bridge/provider binaries in trusted config, require job ownership, and restrict `context_file`/`cwd` to approved repo roots or a staged-input directory."},{"severity":"medium","type":"sensitive_data_exposure","description":"Fallback/status improvements currently persist raw operational data broadly: task text, context file paths, provider state, fallback reasons, raw `stdout`/`stderr`, and synthesized summaries are written to JSON/JSONL/Markdown artifacts under `state/` and `run-spool` (`kernel-lane-bridge.mjs` around lines 171-216 and 1249-1277; runner around lines 121-137, 174-214, and 239-335). No hardcoded credentials were found in the code reviewed, but this logging design can expose secrets that appear in prompts, errors, or provider output.","remediation":"Add redaction for tokens/paths/provider auth errors, default artifact permissions to owner-only, separate debug logs from normal status files, and avoid persisting raw model/provider stderr unless an explicit debug flag is enabled."},{"severity":"low","type":"xss","description":"Human-readable status artifacts interpolate unescaped task text, failure reasons, provider summaries, and file paths into `summary.md` and related JSON (`scripts/local/run-kernel-orchestration.sh` around lines 178-214 and 273-335). In terminal-only local use this is low risk, but if these files are later rendered in GitHub Pages, a dashboard, or any HTML-capable viewer, malicious markdown/HTML from a task or provider error can become stored XSS or content spoofing.","remediation":"Escape markdown/HTML before rendering summaries, keep provider-controlled text out of rich views by default, and sanitize all task/provider strings at the presentation layer."}],"summary":"Assumption: scope is this repository only; external delegate scripts and third-party CLIs were not audited. No direct SQL injection, CSRF surface, or hardcoded secret was found here. The main remaining backlog risk is boundary hardening: the current local runner assumes a trusted local operator, but the planned local runner fallback/status evolution toward qu...
